name: Fyrox
description: Full 3D/2D engine with a native visual scene editor and resource manager (formerly rg3d)

kind.comment: Full 2D/3D engine with a native scene editor and resource manager; markets itself as "like Godot/Unity, but in Rust".

documentation.comment: Official Fyrox Book (mdBook); API docs also at docs.rs/fyrox.

first-release.comment: First published on crates.io as rg3d on 2019-11-02; renamed to Fyrox, first fyrox crate published 2022-01-10.

latest-stable-version.comment: Latest stable on crates.io as of 2026-07-06; 1.0.0 was the milestone release on 2026-03-29.

maintenance-status.comment: 1.0 released March 2026 after seven years of continuous development; regular commits and releases.

maturity.comment: Reached a 1.0 "production-ready" release in 2026 after seven years; API stabilized, but few widely-documented commercially shipped titles, so stable rather than mature.

backing.value: Small team led by a single lead maintainer (Dmitry Stepanov), funded via donations/Patreon; described as having little to no funding
backing.comment: 1.0 blog notes the team is "quite small with little to no funding".

implementation.comment: Written in Rust (~98%); no wrapped non-Rust engine. Rendering via OpenGL bindings (glow) as platform glue.

architecture.comment: Dominant model is a classic node-based scene graph (node tree with prefabs), not ECS.

built-in-ecs.comment: Node/scene-graph based with Rust script components attached to nodes; not an ECS-first engine.

scripting.comment: Gameplay logic written in Rust as script components / dynamic plugins; supports hot-reloading. No Lua/visual scripting.

data-driven-scenes.comment: Scenes and prefabs are authored in the editor, serialized to native scene files, and loaded at runtime.

reflection-serialization.comment: First-class reflection system plus the Visit trait for (de)serialization of game/engine state; powers the editor property inspector.

hot-reload.comment: Hot-reloading of scripts / dynamic plugins, plus asset hot-reload via the resource manager.

visual-editor.comment: Ships FyroxEd, a native graphical scene editor plus a project manager (PC only).

live-inspector.comment: First-party editor property inspector; can inspect/edit node properties while the scene runs in the editor.

asset-pipeline.comment: Resource manager with async import of many formats (FBX/glTF/GLB models, textures, sound), reimport and hot reload — matches the guideline's level-5 (Fyrox resource manager).

cli-project-tooling.comment: fyrox-template CLI scaffolds new projects from templates; 1.0 added a CLI for project export (PC/WASM/Android) for CI/CD.

debug-profiling-tools.comment: Editor exposes rendering/frame statistics and diagnostics; no first-party Tracy or dedicated built-in profiler integration documented.

ide-support.comment: Nothing beyond standard Rust tooling — rust-analyzer works; no dedicated IDE plugin. The Fyrox editor is a scene editor, not a code IDE.

rendering-backend.comment: OpenGL 3.3+ backend (fyrox-graphics-gl / glow) on desktop and Android; WebGL 2.0 on the web (WASM). Not wgpu-based.

pbr.comment: Physically-based rendering pipeline with a material system.

lighting.comment: Deferred shading renderer with a forward pass for transparent objects; dynamic lights with PCF-filtered shadows.

shadows.comment: Real-time PCF-filtered shadow mapping (1.0 increased the PCF kernel size for smoother shadows).

custom-shaders.comment: Custom materials/shaders via the engine's shader/material system.

sprites-tilemaps.comment: Built-in 2D sprites and a tile map system/editor.

post-processing.comment: Post effects including bloom, HDR tonemapping and FXAA.

text-rendering.comment: Built-in font/text rendering via the fyrox-ui system.

physics.comment: Rapier (3D + 2D) integrated natively into the scene graph as built-in physics; 1.0 upgraded to Rapier 0.32.

audio.comment: Built-in Rust audio engine (fyrox-sound) with spatial/HRTF sound.

input.comment: Keyboard/mouse on desktop and touch on mobile/web via raw window events; no prominent first-party gamepad abstraction documented.

animation.comment: Skeletal animation with an Animation Blending State Machine (ABSM) editor; also sprite-sheet animation for 2D.

ui-system.comment: Built-in retained-mode UI framework (fyrox-ui) used for both in-game UI and the editor itself.

networking.comment: No first-party networking/replication; multiplayer projects (e.g. RustCycles) use standard Rust networking crates.

gltf-model-import.comment: Loads FBX natively and glTF/GLB via the gltf feature.

macos.comment: Supported (engine + editor); docs note poor GPU performance on Intel Macs, M1+ works well.

web-wasm.comment: Compiles to WebAssembly (WebGL 2.0) and runs in the browser; engine only, no editor on web.

ios.comment: Only theoretical support per the docs — untested and not officially supported.

android.comment: Official build target (engine only, no editor); android is one of the supported export targets.

consoles.comment: No documented console (Switch/PlayStation/Xbox) support path; docs cover only PC, web and mobile.

clean-build-time.comment: Not measured in this pass; RESEARCH.md requires a consistent measured build to record a value.

incremental-build-time.comment: Not measured in this pass; prefer null over an unverified estimate. Note: script/plugin hot-reload reduces the need for full rebuilds.

binary-size.comment: Not measured in this pass (no stripped minimal-app build performed).

web-bundle-size.comment: Web-capable (WASM/WebGL2) but the minimal-app WASM bundle was not measured in this pass.

runtime-overhead.comment: Not measured; RESEARCH.md says prefer null over a guess for idle CPU/memory.

github-stars.comment: ~9.4k stars on the FyroxEngine/Fyrox repo as of 2026-07-06.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the fyrox crate as of 2026-07-06; the predecessor rg3d crate has ~33k additional downloads.

last-release.comment: Fyrox 1.0.0 released 2026-03-29; a 1.0.1 patch has since been published on crates.io (exact date not confirmed).

shipped-titles.value: Mostly tech demos and open-source projects: Station Iapetus (sci-fi horror demo by the engine author), RustCycles, Fish Folly, rusty-shooter
shipped-titles.comment: No major commercially shipped title is widely documented; listed titles are largely tech demos / open-source games, several by the engine author.

community-size.comment: Active Discord and steady contributions, but development is dominated by a single lead maintainer with a small team — borderline small/medium.

plugin-asset-ecosystem.comment: Batteries-included engine, but a limited third-party plugin/asset ecosystem compared with Bevy.

learning-resources.comment: Official Fyrox Book with tutorials (e.g. RPG series) and many examples, plus some video coverage; video ecosystem is modest compared to Bevy's, so 4 rather than 5.
